transcript.text: I feel like we've been going back and forth in linear and Slack for a while now.
I think for what we're trying to do in Looker, oh, Lindsay just hopped on.
Hi, Aida, can you hear me?
So, yeah, I have Talal here.
He is our resident technical SEO expert and also analytics expert, and he has been customizing your Looker dashboard for these past couple of weeks.
And yeah, I just wanted to get on a call with you to get a better understanding of what you want to see in this view.
I'm going to go ahead and share my screen so you can just kind of talk through it and we will let you know, you know, how we can do it.
And yeah, what are the next steps?
Could you scroll down a little bit quickly?
Sorry, my Wi-Fi is really going in and out, so I'm going to turn off my camera just so.
I had the same thing yesterday.
What was the last thing you heard?
Today, I thought we could all just get together.
I've shared the view that we've been working on together.
We would just love to hear what you would like to get from this view and just how we can help build that for you and Looker.
Call, I'm sure it's much easier to do this in person than back and forth.
My main concern is I have built and launched paid search for field paid search campaign in the last week of September.
And so, what I would like to do is that paid service is getting to not taking off.
Aida, sorry, Lindsay, but it's like you're coming in and then you're like, it's super choppy, so we're having struggle.
It's just really important to hear everything.
Now we can't, like, we can't.
By the way, for the latest comments she made, she's just reading the chart wrong because clicks aren't higher than sessions because clicks are on the left axis and sessions are on the right axis.
And especially for the chart underneath the last one.
Yeah, the branded clicks and the branded sessions are on the left axis and the branded impressions and ad impressions are on the right axis.
So that's the right way to view it.
I'm at my mom's house and I think the Wi-Fi is not great here.
I have the same thing when I go visit my parents.
So I think as I was saying, like I'm managing paid search and what I want to make sure and be able to show is that.

But the paid search, the spend that we're doing in paid search branded terms is adding to the queries and not taking away from, we're not just cannibalizing from organic paid search terms or, you know, branded terms.
My main point is to prove that it's additive and not, you know, we're not cannibalizing.
And so what I, I think my main thing is what I'd like to do is, is make sure that our impressions are increasing overall and that our clicks total are increasing.

And, and I think my, my main thing with those is that I want to make sure that everything is on the same axis so we can, or, you know, the same relative scale so I can see relative to organic where paid is.
Um, yeah, so like, uh, what changes would you like us to make?
Do the current view that would help you determine that?
Yeah, I think if we, that monthly view, just I think the axes are different.
So they're organic clicks and paid clicks.
So what I'd like to be able to do is be able to look at the relative, you know, maybe even a stacked bar chart or just put these on the same axis rather than different axes.
So I can see, you know, because right now one axis is slightly off from the other, or at least it was, right?
So basically put both of these on the x-axis and remove the axis on the right completely.
Just so we can see, you know, if totally what the difference is, you know, numerically.
And then the other one, I, you know, the weekly view is same deal.
And just the scale is very small all of a sudden.
And then this one, I think what I'd like to do is have impressions.
So it looks like the impressions are lower than the clicks, which doesn't make any sense for organic and paid.
So for this graph, what we're doing is we're comparing two sets of numbers that are wildly different.
So the clicks are in the orders of thousands and the impressions are in the order of tens of thousands.
So what I've done is that I've, the branded clicks and the branded sessions, you need to look at the left Y-axis to view them.
And for the branded impressions and the ads impressions, the right Y-axis is the one that you should be looking at.
So if you hover over any of these numbers, like you can see, like the clicks and the sessions are like 1,000 and 3,000, while the branded impressions and ad impressions are like 44 and 98,000.
They're, like, pegged to the right Y-axis, and the clicks and the binary sessions are pegged to the left Y-axis.
So that's the way to read the graph right now.
Yeah, so what I would like to be able to do is to, like, immediately look at it and see, like, if impressions are going up, are we continuing?

So maybe if we can change the clicks to click-through rate and have that on the separate axis, it would be easier to see, like, as impressions are going up, are we still maintaining our share of clicks, right?
Okay, I can try doing that, but I think, like, adding, like, a third set that's measured completely different might not be super useful.
For reference, I did try tinkering with that, but, like, if that's in percentage, then let me see what I can do, but I might just have to create a new graph, and you would have to super...
If you want to get that view, because otherwise it's going to get messy.
Yeah, you can get rid of the clicks.
So just have impressions and then CTRs.
So that'll tell us, like, as impressions rise, are we losing CTR, you know, so we're not capturing the same proportion of clicks, or are we maintaining the level?
Glad we were able to help.
I think, I don't know if, are you a part of our external channel with Fieldguide, or are you just in a DM with me and Yasin?
I think I'm just in a DM at this point.
Yeah, we can keep it that way.
So I will DM you when.
And the updated version of the report is ready.
And yeah, we can take it from there.
I mean, I don't even know if this kind of thing is in your remit or under scope.
So I really appreciate your partnership with us.
I mean, I think it all is going towards helping management see that, you know, the investment we put in paid and SEO is valuable.
So I really appreciate your partnership.
And we want to, we want to do the same thing.
So it does help us as well.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Refine the Looker dashboard to prove paid search value.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Goal:** Prove the new paid search campaign (launched late Sept) is additive to organic traffic, not cannibalizing it.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
  - [**Action 1:** Unify axes on click-comparison charts to show organic vs. paid clicks on a single, relative scale.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=364.0)
  - [**Action 2:** Replace clicks with Click-Through Rate (CTR) on the impressions chart to show if click share is maintained as impressions grow.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)
  - [**Comms:** Aida will notify Lindsay via DM when the updated dashboard is ready for review.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)

## Topics

### Proving Paid Search Value

  - [**Problem:** The dashboard's dual Y-axes obscure the relative performance of paid vs. organic channels, making it difficult to prove the paid campaign's value.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0)
  - [**Objective:** Demonstrate that paid search spend on branded terms is additive to overall queries and clicks, not cannibalizing organic traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)

### Dashboard Refinements

  - [**Monthly/Weekly Click Charts**](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=364.0)
      - [**Issue:** Dual axes prevent a direct, relative comparison of organic vs. paid clicks.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0)
      - [**Solution:** Unify both metrics on a single Y-axis.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=364.0)
  - [**Branded Impressions & Clicks Chart**](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)
      - [**Issue:** The dual-axis view (impressions vs. clicks) is confusing because the metrics are on vastly different scales (thousands vs. tens of thousands).](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)
      - [**Solution:** Replace clicks with Click-Through Rate (CTR) on a separate axis.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)
          - [**Rationale:** This directly shows if the click share is maintained as impressions increase, proving the campaign's efficiency.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Talal:** Implement the dashboard changes:](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=355.0)
      - [Unify axes on monthly/weekly click charts.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=364.0)
      - [Replace clicks with CTR on the impressions chart.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)
  - [**Aida:** Notify Lindsay via DM when the updated dashboard is ready.](https://fathom.video/share/ZX9os7ZAtaK-5LkTAQMQ5F3EZ2MkVE6y?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)

action_items.description: Update Looker: combine organic+paid clicks on single axis; remove right axis; replace clicks w/ CTR; DM Lindsay when ready
